note: Seed knowledge base of well-known Indian central-government welfare schemes. Eligibility rules are simplified for demo/reasoning purposes -- always confirm current rules against the official source_url before advising a real applicant. The Retrieval Agent supplements this list with live web search for schemes not covered here (e.g. state-specific schemes).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi
schemes.description: Income support of INR 6,000 per year, paid in three equal installments, to all landholding farmer families with cultivable land.
schemes.benefits: INR 6,000/year direct benefit transfer (3 installments of INR 2,000 each).

eligibility.additional_notes: Excludes institutional landholders and higher-income categories such as serving/retired government employees drawing high pensions, income-tax payers, and professionals (doctors, engineers, CAs) registered with professional bodies.

schemes.how_to_apply: Register online at pmkisan.gov.in or through the local Common Service Centre (CSC) / patwari / revenue officer.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awaas Yojana - Gramin
schemes.description: Financial assistance to houseless people and those living in dilapidated houses in rural areas to construct a pucca house.
schemes.benefits: INR 1.2 lakh (plain areas) / INR 1.3 lakh (hilly/difficult areas) financial assistance, plus convergence with MGNREGA for labour and Swachh Bharat Mission for toilet construction.

eligibility.additional_notes: Beneficiaries identified from SECC 2011 housing deprivation data / Awaas+ survey; must not already own a pucca house.

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through the Gram Panchayat or check beneficiary status at pmayg.nic.in.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban
schemes.description: Affordable housing support for urban poor (EWS/LIG/MIG households) through credit-linked subsidy, in-situ slum redevelopment, or affordable housing partnerships.
schemes.benefits: Credit-linked interest subsidy on home loans (varies by income slab) or direct construction assistance for slum redevelopment beneficiaries.

eligibility.additional_notes: Household must not own a pucca house anywhere in India. Income slabs: EWS up to INR 3 lakh, LIG up to INR 6 lakh, MIG up to INR 18 lakh annual household income.

required_documents: Affidavit of no pucca house ownership
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply online at pmay-urban.gov.in or through the Urban Local Body (ULB) office.

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat - Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana
schemes.description: Health insurance cover of INR 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization, for economically vulnerable families.
schemes.benefits: Cashless hospitalization cover up to INR 5,00,000 per family per year at empanelled hospitals.

eligibility.additional_notes: Eligibility based on SECC 2011 deprivation/occupational criteria (mostly rural deprivation categories and identified urban occupational categories); senior citizens 70+ covered under AB Vay Vandana irrespective of income.

schemes.how_to_apply: Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in or visit the nearest Ayushman Bharat empanelled hospital's Pradhan Mantri Arogya Mitra (PMAM) desk / nearest CSC.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana
schemes.description: National mission for financial inclusion providing zero-balance bank accounts with RuPay debit card, overdraft, and insurance benefits.
schemes.benefits: Zero-balance savings account, RuPay debit card with accident insurance cover, overdraft facility up to INR 10,000 after satisfactory account operation.

eligibility.additional_notes: Any Indian citizen without a bank account is eligible; no minimum balance requirement.

required_documents: Any officially valid document (OVD) for KYC
schemes.how_to_apply: Open an account at any bank branch or Bank Mitra (Business Correspondent) outlet.

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (under NSAP)
schemes.description: Monthly pension support for elderly citizens living below the poverty line under the National Social Assistance Programme.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension of INR 200-500+ from central government (many states top up the amount further).

eligibility.additional_notes: Applicant must belong to a household certified as Below Poverty Line by the state/local authority.

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through the Gram Panchayat, Block office, or Social Welfare Department; track status at nsap.nic.in.

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (under NSAP)
schemes.description: Monthly pension support for widows living below the poverty line under the National Social Assistance Programme.
schemes.benefits: Monthly pension of INR 300+ from central government (many states top up the amount further).

eligibility.additional_notes: Applicant must be a widow in a BPL household; from age 80 the beneficiary transitions to IGNOAPS old-age pension rates.

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through the Gram Panchayat, Block office, or Social Welfare Department; track status at nsap.nic.in.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana
schemes.description: Provides free LPG gas connections to women from economically weaker households to replace unsafe cooking fuel.
schemes.benefits: Free LPG connection with first refill and stove often provided free or at subsidized cost; ongoing subsidized cylinder refills.

eligibility.additional_notes: Applicant's household should not already have an LPG connection; priority to SC/ST, PMAY, Antyodaya Anna Yojana and other listed BPL households.

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at the nearest LPG distributor or online at pmuy.gov.in.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana
schemes.description: Maternity benefit program providing cash incentives to pregnant and lactating women for their first living child, to promote health and nutrition.
schemes.benefits: INR 5,000 in installments for the first child, linked to registration, ANC checkups, and child immunization milestones (additional INR 6,000 under related schemes for second child if a girl).

eligibility.additional_notes: Excludes women who are already central/state government employees or receiving similar cash maternity benefit under existing law.

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through the nearest Anganwadi Centre or health facility; track at pmmvy.wcd.gov.in.

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students
schemes.description: Central scholarship covering tuition fees, maintenance allowance, and other charges for SC students studying at post-matriculation/post-secondary level.
schemes.benefits: Reimbursement of tuition/compulsory fees plus monthly maintenance allowance (varies by course and hosteller/day-scholar status).

eligibility.additional_notes: Applicant must belong to Scheduled Caste and be studying in a recognized post-matric (class 11 and above) course.

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply online at the National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in).

schemes.name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for Minority Students
schemes.description: Scholarship for students from notified minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Parsi, Jain) studying in classes 1 to 10.
schemes.benefits: Annual scholarship covering admission/tuition fees and maintenance allowance.

eligibility.additional_notes: Applicant must belong to a notified minority community, be enrolled in classes 1-10, and have scored at least 50% marks in the previous final exam (from class 3 onward).

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply online at the National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in).

schemes.name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
schemes.description: Small savings scheme for the financial security of a girl child, offering a high, government-guaranteed interest rate on deposits made until she turns 21.
schemes.benefits: Government-guaranteed interest (reviewed quarterly), tax benefits under Section 80C, maturity after 21 years or on marriage after age 18.

eligibility.additional_notes: Account can be opened by a parent/legal guardian for a girl child below 10 years of age; maximum two accounts per family (relaxation for twins/triplets).

schemes.how_to_apply: Open an account at any post office or authorized bank branch.

schemes.name: Atal Pension Yojana
schemes.description: Guaranteed pension scheme mainly targeted at workers in the unorganized sector, providing a fixed monthly pension after age 60.
schemes.benefits: Guaranteed monthly pension of INR 1,000 to INR 5,000 after age 60, based on contribution amount and age of joining.

eligibility.additional_notes: Requires a bank/post office savings account; non income-tax payers are eligible to join per current rules.

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through your bank/post office savings account branch or via net-banking/mobile banking app.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Suraksha Bima Yojana
schemes.description: Low-cost accidental death and disability insurance scheme available to bank/post office account holders.
schemes.benefits: INR 2 lakh cover for accidental death/full disability, INR 1 lakh for partial disability, at an annual premium of about INR 20.

eligibility.additional_notes: Requires an active bank or post office savings account with auto-debit consent for the annual premium.

schemes.how_to_apply: Enroll through your bank/post office branch, net-banking, or mobile banking app.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana
schemes.description: Low-cost renewable term life insurance scheme available to bank/post office account holders.
schemes.benefits: INR 2 lakh life cover for death due to any cause, at an affordable annual premium (auto-debited).

eligibility.additional_notes: Cover continues up to age 55 (subject to annual renewal); requires an active bank/post office account with auto-debit consent.

required_documents: Self-declaration of good health (for first-time enrollment after the initial window)
schemes.how_to_apply: Enroll through your bank/post office branch, net-banking, or mobile banking app.

schemes.name: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Rural Livelihood Mission
schemes.description: Promotes self-employment and organization of rural poor, especially women, into Self-Help Groups (SHGs) with access to bank credit and skill training.
schemes.benefits: Revolving fund support, community investment fund, collateral-free bank loans at subsidized interest, and livelihood/skill training for SHG members.

eligibility.additional_notes: Priority given to rural poor women willing to organize into or join a Self-Help Group; identified through participatory poverty assessment.

required_documents: SHG membership record (if already a member)
schemes.how_to_apply: Contact the local Block Mission Management Unit (BMMU) or existing SHG/Village Organization; details at aajeevika.gov.in.

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana
schemes.description: Collateral-free institutional credit to non-corporate, non-farm micro and small enterprises for income-generating activities.
schemes.benefits: Loans up to INR 20 lakh across three categories: Shishu (up to 50,000), Kishor (50,000 to 5 lakh), Tarun (5 lakh to 10 lakh) and Tarun Plus (10 lakh to 20 lakh for previous Tarun borrowers).

eligibility.additional_notes: For non-farm income-generating micro/small enterprises in manufacturing, trading, or services; not applicable to purely agricultural crop-production activities (covered instead under farm credit schemes).

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply at any participating bank, NBFC, or MFI branch, or online via the Udyamimitra portal linked from mudra.org.in.

schemes.name: e-Shram - National Database of Unorganised Workers
schemes.description: National registry of unorganized sector workers that issues a portable e-Shram card, acting as the gateway to social security scheme benefits and accident insurance.
schemes.benefits: e-Shram card with unique UAN, automatic accidental insurance cover under PMSBY (INR 2 lakh death/disability) for registered workers, and a single database used to extend future welfare benefits.

eligibility.additional_notes: For workers in the unorganized sector (construction workers, migrant labour, street vendors, domestic workers, gig/platform workers, etc.) who are not currently an EPFO/ESIC member or an income-tax payer.

schemes.how_to_apply: Register free of cost at eshram.gov.in or through the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC).

schemes.name: Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana
schemes.description: Crop insurance scheme providing financial support to farmers in the event of crop failure due to natural calamities, pests, or diseases.
schemes.benefits: Insurance cover for the sum insured on the notified crop, at a low uniform premium (2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, 5% for commercial/horticulture crops); balance premium subsidized by government.

eligibility.additional_notes: Available to both loanee and non-loanee farmers (owner or tenant/sharecropper) growing notified crops in notified areas; enrollment is voluntary for non-loanee farmers.

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through your bank (for loanee farmers, often automatic), CSC, insurance company agent, or online at pmfby.gov.in.

schemes.name: Antyodaya Anna Yojana
schemes.description: Provides highly subsidized food grains to the poorest of the poor households under the targeted public distribution system.
schemes.benefits: 35 kg of food grains per household per month at highly subsidized rates (rice at INR 3/kg, wheat at INR 2/kg, coarse grains at INR 1/kg, under prevailing NFSA rates).

eligibility.additional_notes: Targeted at the poorest of poor households identified by state governments, including destitute households, landless agricultural laborers, marginal farmers, and similar categories with no assured means of subsistence.

required_documents: Aadhaar card of all household members
schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through the local Food & Civil Supplies office or Fair Price Shop; check status at nfsa.gov.in.

schemes.name: Assistance to Disabled Persons for Purchase/Fitting of Aids and Appliances
schemes.description: Provides persons with disabilities with durable, modern, and scientifically manufactured aids and appliances to improve their functional capabilities and support socio-economic rehabilitation.
schemes.benefits: Free or subsidized assistive devices (wheelchairs, hearing aids, artificial limbs, Braille kits, tricycles, etc.) based on income-linked subsidy slabs.

eligibility.additional_notes: Applicant must hold a valid Unique Disability ID (UDID) / disability certificate of at least 40% disability; full subsidy for monthly household income below INR 22,500, partial subsidy up to INR 30,000.

schemes.how_to_apply: Apply through the District Disability Rehabilitation Centre (DDRC), implementing NGO, or ALIMCO camp; details at disabilityaffairs.gov.in.
schemes.source_name: Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities
